---
title: 'The end of self...'
date: '2020-02-10T15:17:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6566998576310540728'
blogger_filename: '/2020/02/the-end-of-self.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

<div>What if suffering isn't good or evil, it just is. And our response is what matters?</div><div><br></div><div><div>"<i>When he saw the crowds, he went up the mountain. After he sat down his disciples came to him. Then he began to teach them by saying: â€œBlessed are the poor in spirit, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them. â€œBlessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted. â€œBlessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth. â€œBlessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be satisfied. â€œBlessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy. â€œBlessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God. â€œBlessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called the children of God. â€œBlessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them. â€œBlessed are you when people insult you and persecute you and say all kinds of evil things about you falsely on account of me. <b>Rejoice and be glad because your reward is great in heaven, for they persecuted the prophets before you in the same way</b>."</i></div><div><i>Matthew 5:1â€­-â€¬12 NET</i></div><div><i>https://bible.com/bible/107/mat.5.1-12.NET</i></div></div><div><br></div><div>We are so quick to look for physical beauty, strength, education, success... And point these out as the epicenter of virtue.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>But one thing I've learned this past few years, is that suffering produces perseverance, patience, and character.</div><div><br></div><div>If...</div><div><br></div><div>If it's responded to correctly.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Or...</div><div><br></div><div>Suffering could also produce bitterness, selfishness, depression. Wow is me.</div><div><br></div><div>The difference seems to be whether we allow the event to turn is inward to focusing on self or outward to focusing on God.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I do not recognize the man I was this time last year. I expect this time next year I'll be doing things I've been avoiding all my life.</div><div><br></div><div>I never imagined I could say this... But I'm grateful for the character I have today, despite (never because of) the things I had to go through to get here.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I will never be grateful for the trials but I can be grateful for the changes they induced.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>God's first choice is our willing reliance on Him. But reliance on him through any means is sweet.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Selah</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581347845967826-0-abacd21ba428.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581347845967826-0-abacd21ba428.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div>
